tasks.instruction: In the BridgeConfig dataclass add one new integer field: task_timeout_seconds with a default value of 300. Add it after the existing fields. Do not change any other fields or logic.

In build_argument_parser(), add a new --task-timeout integer CLI argument with default 300 and help text 'Max seconds any single subprocess call (Aider or supervisor) may run before being killed. Default: 300.'. In main(), pass task_timeout_seconds=int(args.task_timeout) when constructing BridgeConfig. Also update the AiderRunner constructor call to pass timeout=config.task_timeout_seconds, and the SupervisorAgent constructor call to pass timeout=config.task_timeout_seconds.

Add a timeout parameter (int, default 300) to AiderRunner.__init__ and store it as self._timeout. In the run() method, pass timeout=self._timeout to the subprocess.run() call. Add a new except block for subprocess.TimeoutExpired immediately after the existing except OSError block: kill the process if still running, then return ExecutionResult with succeeded=False, exit_code=-1, stdout='', stderr=f'Aider timed out after {self._timeout}s', command=arguments. Import subprocess is already present.

Add a timeout parameter (int, default 300) to SupervisorAgent.__init__ and store it as self._timeout. In the _run() method, pass timeout=self._timeout to the subprocess.run() call. Add a new except block for subprocess.TimeoutExpired immediately after the existing except OSError block: raise SupervisorError(f'Supervisor timed out after {self._timeout}s — command may be hung or waiting for input.').

At the top of the file add 'import shlex'. In _run_ci_command(), replace the subprocess.run() call that uses shell=True with: parse the command string using shlex.split(self._validation_command) into a list called cmd_parts, then call subprocess.run(cmd_parts, cwd=self._repo_root, capture_output=True, text=True, encoding='utf-8', shell=False, timeout=120, check=False). Add an except block for subprocess.TimeoutExpired after the subprocess.run call that returns ValidationResult(task_id=task_id, succeeded=False, message='CI gate command timed out after 120s', stdout='', stderr=''). Also add an except block for ValueError (raised by shlex.split on malformed input) that returns ValidationResult(task_id=task_id, succeeded=False, message=f'CI gate command could not be parsed: {self._validation_command!r}', stdout='', stderr='').

In the file path validation section (where absolute paths and Windows drive letter paths are rejected), add one more check immediately after those existing checks: using pathlib.Path, check if any part of the path equals '..'. If Path(fp).parts contains '..', raise PlanParseError with message f'Path traversal not allowed in task files: {fp!r}'. The import for pathlib.Path should already exist or add it if missing.

Add 'import shutil' to the imports at the top of main.py. Add a new function run_preflight_checks(config: BridgeConfig, logger: logging.Logger) -> None above the main() function. This function must run exactly three checks in order and raise RuntimeError on any failure: (1) Resolve the base executable name from config.aider_command by splitting on spaces and taking the first token, then use shutil.which() to check it exists — if None, raise RuntimeError with message f'Aider not found: {token!r}. Install it with: pip install aider-chat'; (2) Check (config.repo_root / '.git').exists() — if False, raise RuntimeError with message f'Repo root is not a git repository: {config.repo_root}. Initialise with git init.'; (3) Check shutil.disk_usage(config.repo_root).free > 50 * 1024 * 1024 — if False, raise RuntimeError with message f'Insufficient disk space at {config.repo_root}: less than 50 MB free.'. Log a debug message before each check and an info message 'Pre-flight checks passed.' if all three succeed. In main(), call run_preflight_checks(config, logger) immediately after the BridgeConfig is constructed and before any plan loading or generation.
